rationale: The citing paper analyzes video-ad liking with whole-brain fMRI and explicitly includes memory-related neural processes, while the cited paper is a multimethod/fMRI study on ad memory encoding and retrieval (print vs. digital), so it could be a plausible mechanism/background source. However, the provided evidence includes only a DOI match with no in-text citation context, and the focal outcomes differ (liking vs. format-based memory), so substantive reliance is unclear from current data.
